patch.value: All current scale work (pm-9rxu) targets one workspace. The stated very-long-term vision — millions of items, thousands of concurrent agents — will in practice span many repositories and trackers. Unexplored axis: federation. Questions: workspace discovery in monorepos, stable cross-workspace ID references (workspace-qualified ids), read-side aggregation without merging git histories, claim/author identity across workspaces, and how the SDK exposes multi-workspace clients. This story is exploration-only (spike + ADR), deliberately parked at priority 4 until the SDK-first and scale-out initiatives land the single-workspace primitives it would compose.
As an organization with many repos and thousands of agents, I can federate multiple pm workspaces: cross-workspace context, dependencies, and aggregation

Exploration outcome is an ADR under pm-m2u1 covering: monorepo multi-tracker layout (several .agents/pm roots), cross-workspace item references/dependencies, org-level read aggregation (context/list/search across workspaces), and identity/claim semantics across workspaces; explicitly decides what is out of scope; follow-up implementation items filed only after the ADR is accepted

All current scale work (pm-9rxu) targets one workspace. The stated very-long-term vision — millions of items, thousands of concurrent agents — will in practice span many repositories and trackers. Unexplored axis: federation. Questions: workspace discovery in monorepos, stable cross-workspace ID references (workspace-qualified ids), read-side aggregation without merging git histories, claim/author identity across workspaces, and how the SDK exposes multi-workspace clients. This story is exploration-only (spike + ADR), deliberately parked at priority 4 until the SDK-first and scale-out initiatives land the single-workspace primitives it would compose.

## Prior art (audit #15 follow-up — check existing packages first)
The unbraind org already ships pm-ops: multi-repo fleet operations for pm-cli (scan, policy, verify-release, report across many pm repositories). The federation spike/ADR must start from pm-ops as the read-side aggregation prior art and decide what belongs in core SDK primitives vs the pm-ops package (cross-workspace ids, deps, claims are the axes pm-ops does not cover today).

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Explore multi-workspace federation for organizations with many repositories and agent fleets: cross-workspace context, dependencies, aggregation, identity, and governance without weakening single-workspace guarantees.

author: codex-root
message: Connect federation exploration to graph identity, concurrency, event coordination, scale, principal trust, and portfolio governance

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Relationship rationale: federation is an exploration outcome spanning cross-workspace graph identity, concurrent ownership, event coordination, scale, and principal trust. These links expose prerequisites without prematurely choosing a federation architecture.
